steps.title: Clear fractions to get a quadratic
steps.content: Multiply every term by $148cx$: $$148 = cx^{2} + 148x \implies cx^{2} + 148x - 148 = 0.$$

steps.title: Discriminant shortcut: one solution means \(\Delta = 0\)
steps.content:
Exactly one solution \(\Rightarrow b^{2} - 4ac = 0\). With $a = c$, $b = 148$, $c_{\text{const}} = -148$:
$$148^{2} - 4(c)(-148) = 0$$$$21904 + 592c = 0$$$$c = -37.$$
The answer is -37.
